steps.label: Infinite Series
steps.bodyHtml: An infinite series is the sum in order of the elements of a sequence. Consider the sequence $\{a_n\}_{n=1}$ then the series will be given by $$\displaystyle\Sigma_{n=1}^{\infty}a_n=a_1+a_2+a_3+\cdots$$

steps.label: Partial Sums
steps.bodyHtml:
For each positive integer k, the sum
  \[S_k=\Sigma_{n=1}^k a_k=a_1+a_2+\cdots+a_n\]is called the k-th partial sum. The partial sums form a sequence \(\{S_k\}\) if this sequence converges, the sum converges, if not, the sum diverges.

steps.label: Reindex
steps.bodyHtml: Notice that the sum don't need to start at index 0 it could start at any index
